references.text: Beusekom, CF van & Th. PM van de Water. 1989. Sabiaceae. Flora Malesiana series 1, 10: 679-715
Beusekom, CF van. 1971. Revision of Meliosma (Sabiaceae) section Lorenzanea excepted; living and fossil, geography and phylogeny. Blumea 19: 335-529

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 9 November 2025

species.citation: Blume , Cat. Gew. Buitenzorg (1823) 32 ; Beus. & Water, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 704. var. lanceolata. Distribution: Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nicobar Isls, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand. MINDORO. Native.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nicobar Isls, Philippines, Sumatra, Thailand. MINDORO.
species.notes: Collected once by Coode 5364 (L) in 1986 from a steep forested valley near or around the Subaan River, San Teodoro, Oriental Mindoro. Low elevation to 1500(-2900)m.

species.citation: Blume , Rumphia 3 (1847) 199 ; Beus. & Water, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 695. ssp. vulcanica (Merr.) Beusekom, Blumea 19 (1971) 460; Beus. & Water, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 697; ≡ Meliosma vulcanica Merr., PJS 11c (1916) 15; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 518. = Machilus nervosa Merr., PJS 4c (1909) 262; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 189. = Meliosma bontocensis Merr., PJS 20 (1922) 403; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 517. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Apayao, Aurora, Benguet, Mountain Province, Sorsogon, MINDORO, PALAWAN. Notes: Primary rainforests. Low elevation to 2000m. Native.
species.distribution: Java, Philippines, Sumatra. LUZON: Apayao, Aurora, Benguet, Mountain Province, Sorsogon, MINDORO, PALAWAN.

species.citation: (Roxb.) Maxim. , Bull. Acad. Imp. Sci. Saint-Pétersbourg, 3rd Sér. 12 (1867) 64 ; Beus. & Water, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 707; ≡ Millingtonia pinnata Roxb. , Fl. Ind. (Carey & Wallich) 1 (1820) 103. ssp. barbulata (Cufod.) Beusekom ex Welzen, Thai Forest Bull., Bot. 32 (2004) 168; ≡ Meliosma rhoifolia ssp. barbulata Cufod., Oesterr. Bot. Z. 88 (1939) 254; Type: from China (syn). = Meliosma pinnata ssp. arnottiana (Wight) Beusekom, Blumea 19 (1971) 499, nom. illeg.; Beus. & Water, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 697; ≡ Millingtonia arnottiana Wight, Ill. Ind. Bot. (Madras) 1 (1839) 144. = Meliosma luzonensis Merr., Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 29 (1905) 24; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 517. = Meliosma multiflora Merr., Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 29 (1905) 24; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 517. = Meliosma apoensis Elmer, LPB 10 (1939) 3785, nom. inval., no Latin descr. = Meliosma cannarioides Elmer, LPB 10 (1939) 3785, nom. inval., no Latin descr. Distribution: Bangladesh, Cambodia, China, India, Japan, Java, Korea, Laos, Malay Peninsula, Myanmar, Nepal, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Tibet, Vietnam. BATAN, LUZON: Benguet, Ifugao, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur. Native. ssp. macrophylla (Merr.) Beusekom, Blumea 19 (1971) 510; Beus. & Water, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 711; ≡ Meliosma macrophylla Merr., PJS 7c (1912) 294; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 517. = Meliosma macrocarpa Elmer, LPB 10 (1939) 3786, nom. inval., no Latin descr. = Meliosma megalobotrys Merr., PJS 11c (1916) 16; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 517. Distribution: Borneo, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi. LEYTE, LUZON: Benguet, Laguna, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur, PALAWAN. Native. ssp. pendula (Merr.) Beusekom, Blumea 19 (1971) 512; Beus. & Water, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 712; ≡ Meliosma pendula Merr., Publ. Bur. Sci. Gov. Lab. 29 (1905) 25; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 518. = Meliosma macgregorii Merr., PJS 10c (1915) 37; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 517. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Apayao (photos), Benguet, Ifugao, Mountain Province. Notes: Montane mossy forests, ravines and exposed ridges, 1800-2500m. Native. ssp. ridleyi (King) Beusekom, Blumea 19 (1971) 505; Beus. & Water, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 710; ≡ Meliosma ridleyi King, J. Asiat. Soc. Bengal, Pt. 2, Nat. Hist. 65 (1896) 458. = Meliosma paucinervia Merr., PJS 10c (1915) 39; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 518. Distribution: Borneo, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra. LUZON, MINDANAO: Zamboanga, Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO. Native. ssp. sylvatica (Elmer) Beusekom, Blumea 19 (1971) 513; Beus. & Water, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 712; ≡ Meliosma sylvatica Elmer, LPB 2 (1908) 492; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 518. = Meliosma acuminatissima Merr., PJS 10c (1915) 36; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 517. = Meliosma brachybotrys Merr., PJS 12c (1917) 275; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 517. Distribution: Philippines, Sulawesi. LUZON: Aurora, Quezon, MINDORO, NEGROS. Notes: Lowland forests, usually below 750m, growing in the shrub layer. Native.
species.distribution: BATAN, LEYTE, LUZON: Abra (photos), Apayao (photos), Aurora, Benguet, Ifugao, Laguna, Mountain Province, Quezon, Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato (photos), Davao del Norte (photos), Davao del Sur, Misamis Occidental, Zamboanga del Norte (photos), Zamboanga del Sur, MINDORO, NEGROS, PALAWAN.

species.citation: (Roxb.) Walp. , Repert. Bot. Syst. (Walpers) 1 (1842) 103 ; Beus. & Water, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 697; ≡ Millingtonia simplicifolia Roxb. , Pl. Coromandel 3 (1820) 48. ssp. rigida (Siebold & Zucc.) Beusekom, Blumea 19 (1971) 473; Beus. & Water, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 699; ≡ Meliosma rigida Siebold & Zucc., Abh. Math.-Phys. Cl. Königl. Bayer. Akad. Wiss. 4(2) (1845) 153. = Meliosma loheri Merr., PJS 10c (1915) 38; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 517. Distribution: China, Japan, Laos, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Taiwan, Vietnam. LUZON: Mountain Province. Notes: Evergreen broad-leaved or laurophyllous forests. 1200-1600m. Native. ssp. fruticosa (Blume) Beusekom, Blumea 19 (1971) 477; Beus. & Water, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 701; ≡ Meliosma fruticosa Blume, Rumphia 3 (1847) 198. = Meliosma monophylla Merr., PJS 4c (1909) 286; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 517. Distribution: Borneo, Java, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand. LUZON: Rizal. Native.
species.distribution: LUZON: Mountain Province, Rizal, MINDANAO: Zamboanga del Norte (photos), MINDORO.

species.citation: (Jack) Walp. , Ann. Bot. Syst. 1 (1848) 135 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 518; Beus., Blumea 19 (1971) 485; Beus. & Water, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 702; ≡ Millingtonia sumatrana Jack , Malayan Misc. 2 (1822) 30.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sulawesi, Sumatra. MINDANAO: Agusan del Norte, Agusan del Sur, Bukidnon, Cotabato, Davao del Sur, Lanao, Lanao del Sur, South Cotabato, Surigao del Norte, Surigao del Sur, PALAWAN.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 26 June 2025

species.citation: Wall. , Fl. Ind. (Carey & Wallich) 2 (1824) 310 ; Water, Blumea 26 (1980) 48. ssp. philippinensis (C.B.Rob.) Water, Blumea 26 (1980) 50; Beus. & Water, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 687; ≡ Sabia philippinensis C.B.Rob., Bull. Torrey Bot. Club 35 (1908) 70; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 516. Distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Abra, Benguet, Mountain Province. Notes: Forests. Fl. mainly Feb-Apr. ?1100-2100m. Native.
species.distribution: China, Myanmar, Nepal, Philippines, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Abra, Benguet, Mountain Province, MINDORO.

species.citation: Blume , Mus. Bot. 1 (1851) 370 ; Beus. & Water, Blumea 26 (1980) 51; Fl. Males. ser. 1, 10 (1989) 688. = Sabia reticulata Elmer , LPB 2 (1909) 579 ; Merrill, EPFP 2 (1923) 516.
species.distribution: Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Solomon Isls. CATANDUANES, CEBU (photos), LUZON: Camarines Norte (photos), Quezon (photos), Sorsogon, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur, Lanao del Sur, NEGROS.
